sections.title: SCOPE
sections.text: This Code contains a series of requirements and practices to be observed in the preparation and sale, in the street, of foods and beverages for direct consumption. The Code applies to the places where these are prepared, to the points of sale and to the means of transport used.

sections.title: OBJECTIVES
sections.text: The purpose of the Code is to ensure that foods are safe and fit for consumption, thereby protecting the health of persons who use this form of food facility. The Code is based on the Recommended International Code of Practice - General Principles of Food Hygiene - CXC 1-1969 (Rev. 3-1997).

sections.title: PURCHASE OF INPUTS AND INGREDIENTS
sections.parent_title: SECTION 4 - REQUIREMENTS FOR INPUTS AND INGREDIENTS
path_titles: SECTION 4 - REQUIREMENTS FOR INPUTS AND INGREDIENTS
sections.text: Purchase inputs and ingredients from authorized retailers or approved sources, under adequate storage conditions, with refrigeration/freezing when necessary (perishable items), stored on shelves, in boxes or baskets and protected from contamination.  Purchase packaged inputs and ingredients that bear a manufacturer’s name or guarantee.  Do not purchase food products that are unlabelled and/or without a clearly stated shelf life, where appropriate. Only purchase inputs and ingredients whose organoleptic properties are proper or correspond to the specific characteristics or nature of the fresh food or raw material and that do not show signs of any kind of alteration and/or adulteration. Only purchase perishable inputs and ingredients maintained at adequate temperature. Only purchase food products in quantities that correspond to adequate storage/preservation capacity.

sections.title: TRANSPORT, RECEPTION AND STORAGE OF INPUTS AND INGREDIENTS
sections.parent_title: SECTION 4 - REQUIREMENTS FOR INPUTS AND INGREDIENTS
path_titles: SECTION 4 - REQUIREMENTS FOR INPUTS AND INGREDIENTS
TRANSPORT, RECEPTION AND STORAGE OF INPUTS AND INGREDIENTS
sections.text: Transport all purchased inputs and/or ingredients in adequate conditions, avoiding hazards of chemical, physical or biological contamination and spoilage of the goods, maintaining an adequate temperature, and isolating each item to prevent cross-contamination.  They should not be transported together with toxic and/or chemical substances (disinfectants, detergents, pesticides, etc).  For packaged products, follow the manufacturer's instructions on the label; and/or storage conditions complying with general principles of food hygiene and legal provisions in force. The vehicle, containers and packaging used for transportation should be adequately cleaned and disinfected and should have sufficient internal space and the necessary equipment to ensure food safety and quality. Inputs and ingredients must be received and kept in clean, protected places: meat, offal, fish and other perishable products should be placed on trays, under refrigeration and/or freezing, and bulk commodities in clean containers. Shelves, boxes and storage space should be of non-contaminating material, clean and protected from dust and other contaminating agents. Keep recipients containing foods clearly labelled and/or identified and in separate areas from non-edible products like soap, disinfectants, pesticides and other toxic or poisonous substances. Rotate the stock of products on a first in, first out basis. Keep the food protected from contamination by pests (insects, rodents) and other biological agents.

path_titles: SECTION 4 - REQUIREMENTS FOR INPUTS AND INGREDIENTS
sections.text: Purchase inputs and ingredients from authorized retailers or approved sources, under adequate storage conditions, with refrigeration/freezing when necessary (perishable items), stored on shelves, in boxes or baskets and protected from contamination.

sections.parent_title: TRANSPORT, RECEPTION AND STORAGE OF INPUTS AND INGREDIENTS
path_titles: SECTION 4 - REQUIREMENTS FOR INPUTS AND INGREDIENTS
TRANSPORT, RECEPTION AND STORAGE OF INPUTS AND INGREDIENTS
sections.text: Transport all purchased inputs and/or ingredients in adequate conditions, avoiding hazards of chemical, physical or biological contamination and spoilage of the goods, maintaining an adequate temperature, and isolating each item to prevent cross-contamination.  They should not be transported together with toxic and/or chemical substances (disinfectants, detergents, pesticides, etc).  For packaged products, follow the manufacturer's instructions on the label; and/or storage conditions complying with general principles of food hygiene and legal provisions in force.

sections.title: Indoor areas
path_titles: SECTION 5 - REQUIREMENTS FOR THE AREA OR PLACE OF PREPARATION
sections.text:
5.1.1.1 These should be designed and constructed in accordance with Section IV of the Recommended International Code of Practice – General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969, Rev. 3-1997).  
5.1.1.2 They should be sufficiently lit, kept as clean as possible at all times and situated far from any source of contaminants (rubbish, waste water, animals). 
5.1.1.3 Equipment should be made from appropriate materials for easy cleaning and disinfecting, as often as necessary. 
5.1.1.4 Water for general purposes (washing inputs and recipients) should be potable.  
5.1.1.5 Containers for water storage should be cleaned as often as necessary. 
5.1.1.6 Waste water must be adequately disposed of and not present a hazard to potable water, food, surrounding area or food handlers. 
5.1.1.7 The working area, including surfaces in contact with food (utensils, table surface, cutters, water outlets, etc.) s hould be in good condition and properly maintained and should be washed with potable water and disinfected as appropriate. 
5.1.1.8 Do not use, for food, containers previously used for substances that are toxic or harmful to human health, such as insecticides, paints or motor oil. 
5.1.1.9 Do not keep fuel, toxic substances or inflammable products in direct contact with food.  Respect rules of safety regarding electricity and toxic, explosive and inflammable substances in order to protect persons.

sections.title: Outdoor areas
path_titles: SECTION 5 - REQUIREMENTS FOR THE AREA OR PLACE OF PREPARATION
sections.text:
5.1.2.1 These should be sufficiently clean and protected from direct sun, dust and wind, and should not be in direct contact with the public and consumers. 
5.1.2.2 Equipment, such as stoves or similar appliances, must be constructed and maintained  safely and adequately.  
5.1.2.3 The potable water supply should be sufficient for all needs.  When not from a public source of potable water, the water must be of similar quality.  
5.1.2.4 The area where the food is prepared should be at least 60 to 70 cm off the ground and suitable for the quantity of food prepared, handled and kept.  
5.1.2.5 Equipment, utensils, water outlets, working surfaces and other components should meet the same requirements specified in 5.1.1.

sections.title: Hygiene facilities
path_titles: SECTION 5 - REQUIREMENTS FOR THE AREA OR PLACE OF PREPARATION
sections.text:
5.1.3.1 Transported potable water should be kept in an appropriate container (easy to clean, non - toxic material, hermetically sealed, fitted with a cover and tap or stopcock) built in such a manner as to preserve the product and prevent contamination.

5.1.3.2 Containers, utensils and working surfaces for food handling should be of a non-toxic material, easy to clean and resistant to high temperatures when used for cooking.  
5.1.3.3 Detergents and disinfectants used to clean and disinfect working surfaces, utensils, water outlets and other components should, as far as possible, be non-toxic and non-corrosive (Ad. I CXC 1-1969, Rev. 3-1997).

sections.title: NOTE CCP - CLEAN AND DISINFECT ALL SURFACES THAT HAVE BEEN IN CONTACT WITH FRESH MEAT OR FISH TO PREVENT CONTAMINATION OF READY-TO-EAT FOOD. AVOID EXCESSIVE EXPOSURE AT ROOM TEMPERATURE OF FRESH MEAT OR FISH TO PREVENT EXCESSIVE MULTIPLICATION OF PATHOGENIC BACTERIA. sections.title: NOTE CCP - SELECTION, RINSING, WASHING AND DISINFECTING ARE IMPORTANT AND INDISPENSABLE MEASURES THAT CAN BE CARRIED OUT BEFORE STORAGE (TO PREVENT CONTAMINATION IN REFRIGERATORS AND OTHER STORAGE AREAS) OR IMMEDIATELY BEFORE USE. AVOID LEAVING EXCESSIVE WATER ON THE PRODUCT TO PREVENT BACTERIAL MULTIPLICATION AND FOR BETTER STORAGE OF THE PRODUCT. SELECT PLACE OF PURCHASE AND ORIGIN TO ENSURE HAZARDS ARE UNDER CONTROL.

sections.raw_id: path_titles::note ha - fresh meat or fish ...::lvl0
sections.title: NOTE HA - FRESH MEAT OR FISH CAN BE CONTAMINATED FROM ORIGIN AND BY HANDLING/MARKETING CONDITIONS AND CAN PRESENT PATHOGENIC BACTERIA, VIRUSES AND PARASITES, THEREBY ACTING AS A POTENTIAL SOURCE OF CONTAMINATION (SURFACES, FOOD HANDLERS, UTENSILS, ETC). INADEQUATE THAWING CAN LEAD TO PATHOGENIC BACTERIAL MULTIPLICATION ON THE PRODUCT SURFACE, SINCE THIS WILL BE AT ROOM TEMPERATURE FOR A PROLONGED PERIOD OF TIME.

sections.title: NOTE HA - FRUITS AND VEGETABLES CAN BE CONTAMINATED AT SOURCE AND/OR BY CROSS-CONTAMINATION IN THE MARKET (CHILLING WATER, CONTACT WITH SURFACES AND OTHER PRODUCTS), WITH PATHOGENIC BACTERIA, VIRUSES AND PARASITES. TAKE CARE NOT TO CONTAMINATE THE SURFACE OF THE PRODUCT AND/OR THE FINAL PRODUCT. OTHER CONTAMINANTS OF A CHEMICAL NATURE SHOULD BE CONTROLLED AT SOURCE AND DURING TRANSPORT AND STORAGE, SINCE THERE IS NO OTHER EFFECTIVE PREVENTIVE MEASURE THAT CAN BE APPLIED DURING FINAL PREPARATION. PHYSICAL HAZARDS CAN BE CONTROLLED BY MANUAL SEPARATION.

sections.title: NOTE HA  - MICROORGANISMS ARE SENSITIVE TO HEAT TO A DEGREE DEPEND ING ON BIOLOGICAL TYPE AND ON FORM AND DURATION OF EXPOSURE AT DETRIMENTAL TEMPERATURES.
path_titles: NOTE HA  - MICROORGANISMS ARE SENSITIVE TO HEAT TO A DEGREE DEPEND ING ON BIOLOGICAL TYPE AND ON FORM AND DURATION OF EXPOSURE AT DETRIMENTAL TEMPERATURES.
sections.citation: 24cfaeb0-eeaf-4216-b4c7-3e5c7afc13bb, NOTE HA  - MICROORGANISMS ARE SENSITIVE TO HEAT TO A DEGREE DEPEND ING ON BIOLOGICAL TYPE AND ON FORM AND DURATION OF EXPOSURE AT DETRIMENTAL TEMPERATURES., p. 6
sections.text: HOWEVER, COOKING IN KITCHENS IS NOT SUFFICIENT TO STERILIZE FOODS.  THE REMAINING BACTERIA CAN MULTIPLY EXPONENTIALLY AT ROOM TEMPERATURE AND THEIR FINAL NUMBER WILL DEPEND ON THE TIME OF EXPOSURE AT INADEQUATE TEMPERATURE.  MULTIPLICATION IS REDUCED BELOW 5C IN SUCH A WAY AS TO AVOID HIGH NUMBERS OF PATHOGENIC BACTERIA WITH THE SAME INTENSITY AND HIGH LEVEL OF RISK. OTHER FACTORS CAN CONTROL/INTENSIFY THE EFFECT OF H EAT: LOW pH, HIGH ACIDITY, HIGH CONCENTRATION OF SALT, PRESENCE OF ADDITIVES, ETC. (LOW HUMIDITY IS USED TO CONTROL BUT NOT STRENGTHEN HEAT EFFECT).  OTHERS PATHOGENS, SUCH AS FISH PARASITES, CAN BE CONTROLLED BY FREEZING DURING ADEQUATE PERIODS OF TIME PRIOR TO USE.  SOME BACTERIA CAN PRODUCE HEAT-STABLE TOXINS IN THE PRODUCT.

sections.title: REQUIREMENTS FOR THE TRANSPORT OF PREPARED FOOD
path_titles: REQUIREMENTS FOR THE TRANSPORT OF PREPARED FOOD
sections.text:
7.1 Vehicles for the transport of prepared foods should have a separate compartment protected from direct sun, wind, dust, rain and other contaminants.
7.2 This compartment of the vehicle should be made of appropriate material for the transportation of food and should be waterproof and easy to clean.
7.3 The transportation of waste materials should be in special duly closed containers, to protect the food.
7.4 Food should be transported in appropriate containers (made of non-toxic material and thoroughly cleaned and disinfected), properly sealed and protected from outside contamination
7.5 The temperature should be controlled, avoiding room-temperature exposure of foods that must be kept hot or refrigerated, when the time of transportation exceeds 1h and/or in conformity with procedures needed to control bacterial multiplication.
7.6 When the transportation time exceeds 2h, the food containers should be placed in thermal boxes.

sections.title: OUTDOOR SALES AREA
sections.text:
8.1.1 The sales stall (kiosk, barrow, mobile stall, etc) should be built of solid, resistant material and should be sufficiently high to be easily sanitized.
8.1.2 The sales stall and its surroundings should be kept clean, free of litter and in good condition.
8.1.3 When not in use, the sales stall should be kept under cover and in the case of a mobile structure should be kept in a clean place.
8.1.4 The outdoor sales area should not be used for any other purpose.
8.1.5 The outdoor sales area should be located in a zone determined by the authorities so that it is protected from contaminants originating from traffic, pedestrians, domestic animals and/or vectors.
8.1.6 The sales area should be free from personal belongings, such as clothes, footwear, blankets, tobacco etc. Avoid contact between personal belongings and the area of food preparation, storage and/or consumption.
8.1.7 Adornments, such as vases with or without flowers or plants and other items may be placed in such a way that they do not represent a source for food contamination.

8.1.4 The outdoor sales area should not be used for any other purpose.
8.1.5 The outdoor sales area should be located in a zone determined by the authorities so that it is protected from contaminants originating from traffic, pedestrians, domestic animals and/or vectors.
8.1.6 The sales area should be free from personal belongings, such as clothes, footwear, blankets, tobacco etc. Avoid contact between personal belongings and the area of food preparation, storage and/or consumption.
8.1.7 Adornments, such as vases with or without flowers or plants and other items may be placed in such a way that they do not represent a source for food contamination.

sentences.original: Purchase inputs and ingredients from authorized retailers or approved sources, under adequate storage conditions, with refrigeration/freezing when necessary (perishable items), stored on shelves, in boxes or baskets and protected from contamination.

classification.sentence: Only purchase food products in quantities that correspond to adequate storage/preservation capacity.
classification.function: Mandate to purchase food products in quantities matching storage capacity
classification.pattern: Only purchase food products in quantities that correspond to adequate storage/preservation capacity.

components.condition: quantities correspond to adequate storage/preservation capacity

classification.justification: Mandates purchase quantity limits; partial match to purchasing obligation templates.

classification.pattern: Imperative verb phrase commanding to keep food protected from contamination by specified agents

sentences.original:
5.1.1.2 They should be sufficiently lit, kept as clean as possible at all times and situated far from any source of contaminants (rubbish, waste water, animals). 
5.1.1.3 Equipment should be made from appropriate materials for easy cleaning and disinfecting, as often as necessary. 
5.1.1.4 Water for general purposes (washing inputs and recipients) should be potable.  
5.1.1.5 Containers for water storage should be cleaned as often as necessary.

pages.title: REGIONAL CODE OF HYGIENIC PRACTICE FOR THE PREPARATION AND SALE OF STREET FOODS
pages.summary: A página apresenta o título principal do documento e o cabeçalho repetitivo. Existem três seções principais indicadas por 'SECTION 1', 'SECTION 2' e 'SECTION 3'. As seções 1 e 2 são de nível 1 e contêm texto explicativo. A seção 3 inclui diversas definições apresentadas como subtítulos em caixa normal sem numeração clara, tratados aqui como nível 2 sob a seção 3. O texto está associado claramente aos títulos e subtítulos correspondentes. Não há indicação de continuidade entre páginas para qualquer seção.

A página apresenta o cabeçalho repetitivo e um nível 1 "SECTION 4 - REQUIREMENTS FOR INPUTS AND INGREDIENTS". O nível 1 contém dois níveis 2: "4.1 PURCHASE OF INPUTS AND INGREDIENTS" e "4.2 TRANSPORT, RECEPTION AND STORAGE OF INPUTS AND INGREDIENTS". Cada nível 2 contém múltiplos níveis 3 numerados (4.1.1 a 4.1.5 e 4.2.1 a 4.2.7) com conteúdo associado claro. A numeração hierárquica é preservada. Não há indicação de continuidade a partir da página anterior, porém o nível 1 continua na próxima página.

A página contém a seção 5 como nível 1 com seu título completo visível e continuidade a partir e para página seguinte. O nível 2 consiste na seção 5.1 com título visível, servindo de agrupador, mas sem texto direto. O nível 3 inclui três subseções numeradas: 5.1.1, 5.1.2 e 5.1.3, cada uma com seu texto associado. Não há indicação de continuidade para essas subseções na página. O cabeçalho da página é preservado e texto de nota é excluído do text_on_page por ser informação de alerta. A estrutura segue a hierarquia numérica e regras de associação textual para uso em RAG.

A página contém a seção 5.1.3 como nível 1 com sua numeração e título parcialmente visíveis e com texto relacionado às subseções 5.1.3.2 e 5.1.3.3, tendo continuidade desde a página anterior. Em seguida, a seção 5.2 HYGIENIC PRACTICE aparece como nível 1, com múltiplas subseções numeradas no nível 2. A seção 5.2 está marcada como continuação da página anterior e com continuidade para a próxima página. O texto das notas foi excluído para preservar foco e limpeza para RAG. O cabeçalho da página está presente no mapeamento, e a estrutura numérica hierárquica foi seguida, garantindo associação correta dos textos às seções correspondentes.

A página apresenta tópicos hierarquizados a partir do item 6.1.1.4.1, com subseções numeradas detalhando práticas higiênicas para frutas, vegetais, carnes frescas e outros alimentos, além de notas de advertência (NOTE HA e NOTE CCP) relacionadas a contaminação e controle. Não há texto adicional detalhado associado diretamente a cada nó nesta página. A estrutura segue o padrão de hierarquia numérica, com níveis de até 4, e o cabeçalho da página está claramente identificado. Todas as seções e subseções aparecem inteiramente nesta página, sem continuidade para outras.

Página 6 do documento CXC 43R-1995 com hierarquia estruturada contendo seções detalhadas dos itens 6.1.3.4 a 6.2.8 e notas técnicas (NOTE HA, NOTE CCP). As seções são numeradas conforme o padrão e apresentam textos associados restritos a cada nó. Há continuidade em algumas notas técnicas ao final da página. Cabeçalho repetitivo identificado. Não há título principal do documento visível na página.

Página contém as seções 7 e 8 do documento CXC 43R-1995, com seus respectivos subtítulos e textos explicativos. A seção 7 continua de página anterior e segue para a próxima página, apresentando subseções claras. A seção 8 inicia na página com um subtítulo 8.1 e textos associados. Cabeçalho da página identificado, sem título principal visível.

Página 8 do documento CXC 43R-1995 contém o cabeçalho identificado e duas seções principais: 8.2 PROTECTION AND SALE OF FOODS e SECTION 9 - HANDLING AND DISPOSAL OF WASTE AND PEST CONTROL, com seus respectivos subtítulos e textos associados. A seção 8.2 continua da página anterior e segue para a próxima, com múltiplas subseções numeradas hierarquicamente (até nível 3) e notas explicativas associadas ao texto interno. A seção 9 inicia na página com subtítulos e segue para a próxima página. Os títulos estão claramente numerados e os textos associados foram corretamente vinculados aos seus nós.

A página 9 contém subseções numeradas 9.2, 9.3 com sua subseção 9.3.1, e 9.4, cada uma com títulos e textos associados claros. Não há indicação de continuidade a partir da página anterior ou para a próxima. O cabeçalho da página destaca o código e a paginação do documento.
